behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between HTTP-body handling, signature verification, and queue-forwarding modules. A unit test of only the HMAC function can pass while middleware/handler wiring still forwards invalid payloads. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires reject/forward logic for one request; it does not require real network/OS effects or subprocesses.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler through an in-process HTTP app pipeline with raw-body middleware enabled, send a payload with an invalid `X-Signature-256`, assert `401`, and assert the queue writer mock/spied adapter was not called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across handler and persistence modules. Mocking persistence as an immediate no-op can hide ordering bugs; test should include the real queue persistence path (local DB/filesystem is still Integration). Rejected higher level: no real external network/subprocess dependency is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process HTTP request and the real persistent queue implementation (or test DB/file backend). Instrument queue write completion and response send time; assert durable write completes before `200` is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is async decoupling between ingestion path and downstream worker module. A single-function unit test can miss wiring where handler accidentally awaits worker completion. Rejected higher level: behavior does not require real OS/network; in-process module wiring with a deliberately slow worker is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Configure downstream processor to block (e.g., unresolved promise until signaled). Send a valid webhook request and assert response is returned promptly while processor is still running; then release processor and verify completion afterward.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time concurrency SLA depending on runtime scheduling and HTTP server behavior under concurrent load. In-process fakes can hide event-loop/backpressure issues that only appear with real timing and socket handling. Rejected lower level (Integration): behavior explicitly requires wall-clock bound under concurrency, which is an infrastructure/timing boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service process (or real server instance), enqueue/trigger slow concurrent downstream work from earlier events, send new valid webhook(s) over real HTTP, and assert end-to-end response latency stays under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Idempotency failure appears at handoff between handler, dedup check, and persistent queue write path. A unit test of dedup logic alone can miss persistence/transactional interaction bugs. Rejected higher level: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; local persistence wiring is enough.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the handler against the real dedup+queue persistence modules. Assert first write succeeds and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is contract mapping between queue module error/state and HTTP response semantics. A pure unit test may miss real cross-module error-shape mismatches. Rejected higher level: text says 'when the queue reports it is full' (already signaled), so this behavior does not require proving real capacity exhaustion mechanics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue module interface that emits the real 'full' condition/error shape; send a valid request and assert `503` plus correct `Retry-After` header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates queue storage behavior against real configured capacity, which is beyond a single-function unit test. Requires real queue module with its local persistence backend; mocks would trivially pass and hide enforcement bugs. Rejected higher level: no requirement for external network/subprocess/OS-specific primitives beyond local DB/filesystem.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small configured capacity, fill it to limit, attempt one more enqueue, and assert rejection/error plus no persisted overflow record.
